desc: Large black jumping spider with green chelicerae and white spots. Common garden predator.
Bright orange-red jumping spider. Males with striking display. In chaparral and scrub.
Tiny colorful jumping spider — male performs elaborate courtship dance. Red face and green legs.
Small dark jumping spider on tree trunks and fences. Common in urban areas.
Elongated spider with huge chelicerae. Builds orb webs near water. Males and females lock jaws during mating.
Striking silver and brown orbweaver with lobate web decorations (stabilimenta).
Small cobweb spider with distinctive zigzag pattern. Common indoors. Preys on other spiders.
Bizarre spider with abdominal spines. Small orb web in woodland understory.
Green spider that hunts on vegetation. Does not build web. Ambushes prey with excellent vision.
Small ground-dwelling orbweaver relative. Found in leaf litter near streams.
Fast, flat spider that hunts actively on ground and bark. Does not build webs.
Specialized predator of other spiders. Invades webs and plucks silk to lure prey.
Orb weaver with silver, yellow, and black banded pattern. Large webs in gardens and fields.
Variable brown orbweaver. Builds new web nightly. Most common large orbweaver in LA.
Golden-blonde tarantula of desert margins. Males wander in fall seeking mates.
Reddish spider with massive chelicerae specialized for piercing isopod armor. Under rocks and pots.
Builds sheet webs with funnel retreat in grass and low vegetation. Very fast. Venom studied for neuroscience.
Slow spider that spits sticky venomous silk to immobilize prey from a distance. Six eyes.
Largest scorpion in NA — to 15cm. Yellow-green with dark back. Desert margins of LA County.
Whip scorpion that sprays acetic acid (vinegar). Nocturnal desert predator. Harmless to humans.
